description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, blocky stone with uneven, jagged surfaces and a mix of gray, blue, and yellowish hues. The stone has a visibly coarse texture, with some areas appearing more weathered and others showing fresher break lines. One face features a handwritten marking in black ink, suggesting it may have been cataloged or sampled for study. The stone's edges are irregular, with some sharp corners and others more rounded, indicating natural fracturing. Subtle veining and color banding are visible, especially along the longer faces, hinting at mineral inclusions or sedimentary layering. The overall appearance suggests a dense, solid rock, likely used as an obstacle or barrier in an urban setting.
description: A dense, blocky stone with jagged edges, gray-blue coloration, and visible veining, featuring a handwritten marking on one face. Its rough, weathered surface and irregular shape make it suitable as an urban obstacle or barrier.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone, with a visible handwritten marking and a mix of gray, blue, and yellowish tones.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the stone's jagged, uneven edge and the transition between blue-gray and yellowish areas.
description_view_2: This side displays a long, flat surface with a distinct horizontal band of blue-gray coloration and a small dark inclusion.
description_view_3: This view reveals a more angular, rough face with visible veining and a lighter, weathered appearance.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a loose barrier
attribute_pattern: Subtle veining and color banding, with irregular mottling and a handwritten marking on one face.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with some fresh break lines, indicating partial exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements in urban settings.
